title: Level 8 Challenge
subtitle: Build and ship a complete project using Claude Code

sections.content:
This is the real deal. You're going to build a **complete bookmark manager application** from scratch using Claude Code — and push it to GitHub when you're done.

No step-by-step hand-holding. No simulations. You have all the skills:

- **Terminal** (Level 2) — navigating and running commands
- **File system** (Level 3) — creating and organizing files
- **Git** (Level 4) — version control and GitHub
- **Web fundamentals** (Level 5) — HTML, CSS, JavaScript
- **Node.js** (Level 7) — building real projects
- **Claude Code** (Level 8) — AI-powered development

Each checkbox below is a feature to add. Build them one at a time using Claude Code. Review every change with `git diff`. Use feature branches. This is the professional workflow.

Take your time. There's no rush. The goal isn't speed — it's building something real that works.

sections.instruction: Build a bookmark manager from scratch using Claude Code. Complete each feature one at a time, in order. Use the iterative workflow you've learned: prompt Claude, review the changes, commit, then move on.

items.text: Create a new project folder and initialize with npm
items.hint: mkdir bookmark-app && cd bookmark-app && npm init -y

items.text: Use Claude to build: HTML page with a form (URL + title + category fields)
items.hint: Describe exactly what fields you want and how the form should look

items.text: Use Claude to add: localStorage persistence
items.hint: Tell Claude: 'Save bookmarks to localStorage and load them on page refresh'

items.text: Use Claude to add: display bookmarks grouped by category
items.hint: Describe the grouping behavior clearly — bookmarks should appear under category headings

items.text: Use Claude to add: search/filter functionality
items.hint: Ask Claude to filter bookmarks as the user types in a search bar

items.text: Use Claude to add: delete and edit capabilities
items.hint: One feature at a time — add delete first, then edit. Iterate!

items.text: Review every change with git diff
items.hint: Don't skip this step — it's how you stay in control of your codebase

items.text: Use git branches for each feature
items.hint: git checkout -b feature-name for each new feature, merge when done

items.text: Push the finished project to GitHub
items.hint: git remote add origin <your-repo-url> && git push -u origin main

sections.question: In the professional Claude Code workflow, what comes after building a feature?
sections.explanation: After building a feature with Claude Code, you should always review the changes with git diff before committing. This ensures you understand and approve everything Claude wrote.

sections.prompt: After Claude Code makes changes to your files, you should always review them using `git _____` before committing.
sections.hint: This git command shows you exactly what changed — green lines added, red lines removed.
sections.successMessage: Correct — git diff shows you every change so you can review before committing.

sections.content:
**Congratulations. You just built and shipped a real application using Claude Code.**

Let that sink in. You started this course not knowing what a terminal was. Now you've:

- Navigated the file system from the command line
- Created and managed files and folders
- Used git for version control
- Understood how websites and servers work
- Built a Node.js project from scratch
- Used an AI pair programmer to build a complete app
- Pushed your work to GitHub for the world to see

You're not just following tutorials anymore. You're **building things**. That's the difference between learning about software and being someone who creates it.

The bookmark manager on your GitHub profile is proof. You built that. With real tools. Using a professional workflow.

And this is just the beginning.
keyPoints: You built a real, working application from scratch
You used Claude Code as a professional development tool
Your project is live on GitHub — a real portfolio piece
Next up: Level 9 — the patterns that junior developers use every day

completionMessage: Level 8 complete! You've gone from learning about Claude Code to using it to build and ship a real project. You're not just learning anymore — you're building. That's what developers do.

milestone.title: Level 8 Complete: Claude Code — Your AI Pair Programmer
summary: Review and iterate on AI-generated code
Combine Claude Code with a professional git workflow
milestone.nextLevelTeaser: Next up: Level 9 — Junior Developer Patterns. You'll learn to debug, deploy, and work like a professional.
